senses.definition: the action of a plan or device failing and causing the opposite result
examples: The policy led to unexpected backfiring.
His rude comment caused backfiring in the meeting.
The marketing strategy resulted in backfiring sales.

senses.definition: a loud noise from an engine when fuel burns unexpectedly
examples: We heard the backfiring of the old car.
The motorcycle's backfiring scared the dog.
Backfiring echoed in the garage.

senses.definition: a young girl in her teens (old-fashioned German term)
examples: She was a typical backfisch, full of energy.
The novel describes a backfisch in 19th-century Germany.

senses.definition: plural of backfisch; young girls in their teens (old-fashioned)
examples: The story featured backfisches exploring the town.
Backfisches gathered at the festival.

senses.definition: to add new safety equipment to an older machine or building
examples: They need to backfit the plane with new parts.
The factory will backfit all machines next year.
We backfit the building to meet codes.

senses.definition: third person of backfit; adds new equipment to older machines
examples: The engineer backfits the device daily.
It backfits old software with updates.

senses.definition: plural of backfit; acts of adding new equipment
examples: Several backfits improved safety.
The backfits cost a lot.

senses.definition: past tense of backfit; added new equipment to older systems
examples: They backfitted the reactor last month.
The car was backfitted with airbags.

senses.definition: the process of adding new parts to older machines or systems
examples: Backfitting is required by law.
The backfitting took weeks.

senses.definition: present participle of backfit; adding upgrades to old systems
examples: They are backfitting the fleet now.
Engineers are backfitting the bridge.

senses.definition: plural of backfitting; processes of upgrading old equipment
examples: Multiple backfittings were completed.
The backfittings enhanced performance.

senses.definition: a jump in the air turning your body backwards once
examples: She did a perfect backflip.
The gymnast practiced backflips all day.
He landed the backflip safely.

senses.definition: a sudden complete change in opinion or policy
examples: The politician's backflip on taxes surprised everyone.
His backflip on the deal angered supporters.

senses.definition: past tense; performed a backward jump or changed opinion suddenly
examples: She backflipped across the mat.
The leader backflipped on his promise.

senses.definition: present participle; doing backward jumps or changing views suddenly
examples: He is backflipping on stage.
They are backflipping their stance now.

senses.definition: plural of backflip; backward jumps or sudden changes
examples: She won with three backflips.
The backflips in policy confused voters.

senses.definition: flow of water or liquid in the reverse direction
examples: Backflow can contaminate the water supply.
Install a valve to prevent backflow.
The pipe had a backflow issue.

senses.definition: flowing backwards, against the normal direction
examples: The backflowing water flooded the street.
Stop the backflowing drain.

senses.definition: plural of backflow; reverse flows of liquid
examples: Multiple backflows damaged the system.
Check for backflows regularly.

senses.definition: a secret enemy who pretends to be a friend (very old use)
examples: Beware the backfriend in your circle.

senses.definition: a furrow plowed in the opposite direction to level land
examples: Farmers use backfurrows for even fields.
The backfurrow smoothed the soil.

senses.definition: to plow a furrow in the reverse direction
examples: They backfurrow the field each season.

senses.definition: a defensive strategy in backgammon using hit pieces behind opponent's
examples: He played a strong backgame.
The backgame caught them off guard.

senses.definition: a board game for two players using dice and pieces
examples: We played backgammon last night.
Backgammon is fun with friends.
She won at backgammon.

senses.definition: past tense; won a backgammon game by opponent's early loss
examples: He backgammoned her in three moves.

senses.definition: present participle; winning backgammon by quick opponent defeat
examples: She is backgammoning him easily.
